title: SACRAMENTO, CA –
prompt_preview:

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "California is one of the most economically unequal states in the country.",
    "The COVID crisis has further exacerbated inequality in California.",
    "The proposed wealth tax would apply a 1% tax on wealth of $50 million or more and 1.5% on wealth in excess of one billion dollars.",
    "Only the top 0.07% of the richest families in California would be affected by the proposed wealth tax.",
    "The proposed wealth tax is estimated to raise about $22.3 billion per year.",
    "Income and wealth are more concentrated in California than in the US overall.",
    "One in four billionaires in the US lives in California.",
    "The collective wealth of California billionaires has surged from $300 billion a decade ago to $960 billion in January 2021.",
    "Since the start of the pandemic, US billionaires have accumulated an additional $1.1 trillion in wealth.",
    "The California income tax is not very effective at taxing the ultra-wealthy.",
    "The wealthy can avoid the California income tax by not selling their stocks.",
    "The proposed wealth tax would tax all wealth, whether this wealth has been realized as income or not.",
    "The ultra-rich have increased their wealth by more than $1.3 trillion since the start of the pandemic.",
    "The California Constitution currently limits the tax rate to 0.4%."
  ]
}
```
